steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
LEFT: An independent clause ending with “and [blank]”
RIGHT: An independent clause that becomes a dependent clause when “though” precedes it

steps.title: Apply the rule for dependent clauses
steps.content:
When a subordinating conjunction like “though” (meaning “even though”) introduces a dependent clause after an independent clause, no punctuation separates them—choice D correctly places no punctuation between “composition” and “though.” Choices A, B, and C each incorrectly add unnecessary or misused punctuation (commas, a colon, or a semicolon).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
